Events Calender About the Masjid Established in 1986, THE ISLAMIC SOCIETY OF MONMOUTH COUNTY (ISMC) is a non-profit organization with the objective of strengthening the bond of humanity. We are dedicated to creating an atmosphere for better citizenship and improving the well being of our community at large. Serving all those in need is a primary goal for our organization. Through our association with several humanitarian relief and development organizations, we are able to respond to human suffering in emergency and disaster situations at home and abroad. Get to Know us Our Services The Islamic Society of Monmouth County is committed to making all services required by the Muslim community easily accessible including but not limited to: Marriage Services Allah says in Qur’an, “And of His signs is that He created for you from yourselves mates that you may find tranquillity in them; and He placed between you affection and mercy. Indeed in that are signs for a people who give thought.” 30:21 Youth Programs Our goal is to engage today’s youth with engaging activities that help with building our future generation. title: #ICPC #FeedTheCommunity #FoodRelief #ServeWithCompassion #CommunitySupport
description: 19 likes, 0 comments - icpcnj on April 22, 2026: "We are pleased to share that ICPC has been granted a special opportunity through the Islamic Relief USA Food Truck Program to serve our community by providing up to 600 hot meals each week. These meals will be prepared and delivered to us fully packaged, allowing for an efficient and dignified distribution process. Beginning Friday, April 24th, meal distribution will take place weekly between Asr and Maghrib at ICPC Paterson. This initiative is open to everyone in the community—there are no eligibility requirements and no questions will be asked. Our goal is simply to ensure that anyone in need has access to a warm meal in a welcoming and respectful environment. In accordance with the program’s grant requirements, we kindly ask that only one meal be taken per person, so that we can serve as many individuals as possible. We encourage you to share this information with others who may benefit. May Allah (SWT) place barakah in this effort and allow it to be a source of benefit for our entire community. #ICPC #FeedTheCommunity #FoodRelief #ServeWithCompassion #CommunitySupport".

title: Our life experience is enriched by the presence of those of wisdom and understanding around us. One of the best ways to avoid misdirection and poor decision-making is by allowing righteous people with experience into our inner circles. No matter how much we grow, we can never grow out of needing good voices in our corners. Join us this Friday as we discuss the framework for mentorship set by our pious predecessors. We hope to see you there!

description: 2 likes, 1 comments - mcgprinceton on April 23, 2026: "MUSLIM YOUTH CIVIC LEADERSHIP INTENSIVE Calling all high school students! Join CAIR-NJ and MCGP for a powerful one-day intensive designed to empower the next generation of leaders. This program offers a comprehensive introduction to civic literacy, leadership development, and how to create meaningful impact at the local level. Led by CAIR-NJ and facilitated by Asma Elhuni, participants will explore advocacy through an Islamic lens under the theme Faith. Voice. Local Power. and gain practical tools to navigate and influence the systems shaping our communities. 🗓️ Sunday, May 3 ⏰ 9:00AM - 5:00PM 📍 The Muslim Center - MP3/379 🔗 Registration: cair-nj.org/pathways 📌 Registration Deadline: April 25".

title: Sunday School
description: The Jam-e-Masjid Islamic Center Inc., was established in May, 1988 as a non-profit religious educational organization to serve the Muslim community. It is a community-based organization depending on its volunteers to enable the development of a model Islamic society based on the guidance from the Quran and the Sunnah of Prophet Muhammad (peace and blessings be upon him) to maximize our reverence towards Allah (most gracious most merciful).

It is a community-based organization depending on its volunteers to enable the development of a model Islamic society based on the guidance from the Quran and the Sunnah of Prophet Muhammad (peace and blessings be upon him) to maximize our reverence towards Allah (most gracious most merciful). The dedicated volunteers work in harmony towards the community’s efforts to: Build and sustain a progressive religious and cultural center for fulfilling our spiritual and social responsibilities. Build strong and cohesive relationships within the community irrespective of ethnicity, race, and religion. Facilitate Islamic education for children and adults. Enhance peace and justice. Develop a culture of caring. Mission Statement Help meet the religious, social, and cultural needs of the Muslim community, via the Sunday School, Alphabet-land, Quranic School, Salat-ul-Jumuah, Al-Ramadan, and Eid Programs. title: Lessons From Surah Yusuf
description: Join us for Friday Halaqa after Isha, as we reflect on lessons from Surah Yusuf. We will explore patience during hardship, trust in Allah during uncertainty, integrity under pressure, forgiveness over resentment, and how Allah’s plan unfolds over time. This session connects the story of Prophet Yusuf to real challenges you face today, including family conflict, temptation, setbacks, and leadership. All are welcome. Come to listen, reflect, and grow together.

Events Calender About the Masjid Established in 1986, THE ISLAMIC SOCIETY OF MONMOUTH COUNTY (ISMC) is a non-profit organization with the objective of strengthening the bond of humanity. We are dedicated to creating an atmosphere for better citizenship and improving the well being of our community at large. Serving all those in need is a primary goal for our organization. Through our association with several humanitarian relief and development organizations, we are able to respond to human suffering in emergency and disaster situations at home and abroad. Get to Know us Our Services The Islamic Society of Monmouth County is committed to making all services required by the Muslim community easily accessible including but not limited to: Marriage Services Allah says in Qur’an, “And of His signs is that He created for you from yourselves mates that you may find tranquillity in them; and He placed between you affection and mercy. Indeed in that are signs for a people who give thought.” 30:21 Youth Programs Our goal is to engage today’s youth with engaging activities that help with building our future generation. title: Sunday School
description: The Jam-e-Masjid Islamic Center Inc., was established in May, 1988 as a non-profit religious educational organization to serve the Muslim community. It is a community-based organization depending on its volunteers to enable the development of a model Islamic society based on the guidance from the Quran and the Sunnah of Prophet Muhammad (peace and blessings be upon him) to maximize our reverence towards Allah (most gracious most merciful).
raw_text: Jam-e-Masjid Islamic Center Skip to content 📞 973-588-4059 📧 info@jmic.org Live Donate School Home Masjid Events Services Imam Appointment Nikkah Education Services Alphabetland Sunday School Quran Learning Institute Community Services Social Services JMIC Assembly Hall Literary Social Media YouTube Channel Facebook Instagram Our App Photo Gallery Donate Funeral Services Contact Live Donate School Home Masjid Events Services Imam Appointment Nikkah Education Services Alphabetland Sunday School Quran Learning Institute Community Services Social Services JMIC Assembly Hall Literary Social Media YouTube Channel Facebook Instagram Our App Photo Gallery Donate Funeral Services Contact Welcome To Jam-e-Masjid Islamic Center The Jam-e-Masjid Islamic Center Inc., was established in May, 1988 as a non-profit religious educational organization to serve the Muslim community. It is a community-based organization depending on its volunteers to enable the development of a model Islamic society based on the guidance from the Quran and the Sunnah of Prophet Muhammad (peace and blessings be upon him) to maximize our reverence towards Allah (most gracious most merciful). The dedicated volunteers work in harmony towards the community’s efforts to: Build and sustain a progressive religious and cultural center for fulfilling our spiritual and social responsibilities. Build strong and cohesive relationships within the community irrespective of ethnicity, race, and religion. Facilitate Islamic education for children and adults. Enhance peace and justice. Develop a culture of caring. Mission Statement Help meet the religious, social, and cultural needs of the Muslim community, via the Sunday School, Alphabet-land, Quranic School, Salat-ul-Jumuah, Al-Ramadan, and Eid Programs. title: Lessons From Surah Yusuf
description: Join us for Friday Halaqa after Isha, as we reflect on lessons from Surah Yusuf. We will explore patience during hardship, trust in Allah during uncertainty, integrity under pressure, forgiveness over resentment, and how Allah’s plan unfolds over time. This session connects the story of Prophet Yusuf to real challenges you face today, including family conflict, temptation, setbacks, and leadership. All are welcome. Come to listen, reflect, and grow together.

Events Calender About the Masjid Established in 1986, THE ISLAMIC SOCIETY OF MONMOUTH COUNTY (ISMC) is a non-profit organization with the objective of strengthening the bond of humanity. We are dedicated to creating an atmosphere for better citizenship and improving the well being of our community at large. Serving all those in need is a primary goal for our organization. Through our association with several humanitarian relief and development organizations, we are able to respond to human suffering in emergency and disaster situations at home and abroad. Get to Know us Our Services The Islamic Society of Monmouth County is committed to making all services required by the Muslim community easily accessible including but not limited to: Marriage Services Allah says in Qur’an, “And of His signs is that He created for you from yourselves mates that you may find tranquillity in them; and He placed between you affection and mercy. Indeed in that are signs for a people who give thought.” 30:21 Youth Programs Our goal is to engage today’s youth with engaging activities that help with building our future generation. title: Sunday School
description: The Jam-e-Masjid Islamic Center Inc., was established in May, 1988 as a non-profit religious educational organization to serve the Muslim community. It is a community-based organization depending on its volunteers to enable the development of a model Islamic society based on the guidance from the Quran and the Sunnah of Prophet Muhammad (peace and blessings be upon him) to maximize our reverence towards Allah (most gracious most merciful).

It is a community-based organization depending on its volunteers to enable the development of a model Islamic society based on the guidance from the Quran and the Sunnah of Prophet Muhammad (peace and blessings be upon him) to maximize our reverence towards Allah (most gracious most merciful). The dedicated volunteers work in harmony towards the community’s efforts to: Build and sustain a progressive religious and cultural center for fulfilling our spiritual and social responsibilities. Build strong and cohesive relationships within the community irrespective of ethnicity, race, and religion. Facilitate Islamic education for children and adults. Enhance peace and justice. Develop a culture of caring. Mission Statement Help meet the religious, social, and cultural needs of the Muslim community, via the Sunday School, Alphabet-land, Quranic School, Salat-ul-Jumuah, Al-Ramadan, and Eid Programs. title: Lessons From Surah Yusuf
description: Join us for Friday Halaqa after Isha, as we reflect on lessons from Surah Yusuf. We will explore patience during hardship, trust in Allah during uncertainty, integrity under pressure, forgiveness over resentment, and how Allah’s plan unfolds over time. This session connects the story of Prophet Yusuf to real challenges you face today, including family conflict, temptation, setbacks, and leadership. All are welcome. Come to listen, reflect, and grow together.

Events Calender About the Masjid Established in 1986, THE ISLAMIC SOCIETY OF MONMOUTH COUNTY (ISMC) is a non-profit organization with the objective of strengthening the bond of humanity. We are dedicated to creating an atmosphere for better citizenship and improving the well being of our community at large. Serving all those in need is a primary goal for our organization. Through our association with several humanitarian relief and development organizations, we are able to respond to human suffering in emergency and disaster situations at home and abroad. Indeed in that are signs for a people who give thought.” 30:21 Youth Programs Our goal is to engage today’s youth with engaging activities that help with building our future generation. title: Sunday School
description: The Jam-e-Masjid Islamic Center Inc., was established in May, 1988 as a non-profit religious educational organization to serve the Muslim community. It is a community-based organization depending on its volunteers to enable the development of a model Islamic society based on the guidance from the Quran and the Sunnah of Prophet Muhammad (peace and blessings be upon him) to maximize our reverence towards Allah (most gracious most merciful).
raw_text: Jam-e-Masjid Islamic Center Skip to content 📞 973-588-4059 📧 info@jmic.org Live Donate School Home Masjid Events Services Imam Appointment Nikkah Education Services Alphabetland Sunday School Quran Learning Institute Community Services Social Services JMIC Assembly Hall Literary Social Media YouTube Channel Facebook Instagram Our App Photo Gallery Donate Funeral Services Contact Live Donate School Home Masjid Events Services Imam Appointment Nikkah Education Services Alphabetland Sunday School Quran Learning Institute Community Services Social Services JMIC Assembly Hall Literary Social Media YouTube Channel Facebook Instagram Our App Photo Gallery Donate Funeral Services Contact Welcome To Jam-e-Masjid Islamic Center The Jam-e-Masjid Islamic Center Inc., was established in May, 1988 as a non-profit religious educational organization to serve the Muslim community. It is a community-based organization depending on its volunteers to enable the development of a model Islamic society based on the guidance from the Quran and the Sunnah of Prophet Muhammad (peace and blessings be upon him) to maximize our reverence towards Allah (most gracious most merciful). The dedicated volunteers work in harmony towards the community’s efforts to: Build and sustain a progressive religious and cultural center for fulfilling our spiritual and social responsibilities. Build strong and cohesive relationships within the community irrespective of ethnicity, race, and religion. Facilitate Islamic education for children and adults. Enhance peace and justice. Develop a culture of caring. Mission Statement Help meet the religious, social, and cultural needs of the Muslim community, via the Sunday School, Alphabet-land, Quranic School, Salat-ul-Jumuah, Al-Ramadan, and Eid Programs. Events Calender About the Masjid Established in 1986, THE ISLAMIC SOCIETY OF MONMOUTH COUNTY (ISMC) is a non-profit organization with the objective of strengthening the bond of humanity. We are dedicated to creating an atmosphere for better citizenship and improving the well being of our community at large. Serving all those in need is a primary goal for our organization. Through our association with several humanitarian relief and development organizations, we are able to respond to human suffering in emergency and disaster situations at home and abroad. Get to Know us Our Services The Islamic Society of Monmouth County is committed to making all services required by the Muslim community easily accessible including but not limited to: Marriage Services Allah says in Qur’an, “And of His signs is that He created for you from yourselves mates that you may find tranquillity in them; and He placed between you affection and mercy. Indeed in that are signs for a people who give thought.” 30:21 Youth Programs Our goal is to engage today’s youth with engaging activities that help with building our future generation. title: Join Shaykh Ismail Bowers for an intimate Eid al-Adha retreat through the spiritual heart of Morocco 🇲🇦
description: 114 likes, 0 comments - buraqtravel.co on February 1, 2026: "Join Shaykh Ismail Bowers for an intimate Eid al-Adha retreat through the spiritual heart of Morocco 🇲🇦 ✦ Fez — home to the world’s oldest university ✦ Meknes — where history and living heritage meet ✦ Ifrane — Eid prayers + celebration in the Atlas Mountains ✦ Casablanca — closing reflections at the iconic Hassan II Mosque 8 days. 50 seats. All-inclusive packages available. Whether you’re drawn by faith, fascinated by history, or simply hungry for something meaningful — this journey is for you. 📅 May 23–30, 2026 🔗 Link in bio or visit iscj.org/morocco #Morocco #EidAlAdha #ISCJ #BuraqTravel".

title: Sunday School
description: The Jam-e-Masjid Islamic Center Inc., was established in May, 1988 as a non-profit religious educational organization to serve the Muslim community. It is a community-based organization depending on its volunteers to enable the development of a model Islamic society based on the guidance from the Quran and the Sunnah of Prophet Muhammad (peace and blessings be upon him) to maximize our reverence towards Allah (most gracious most merciful).
raw_text: Jam-e-Masjid Islamic Center Skip to content 📞 973-588-4059 📧 info@jmic.org Live Donate School Home Masjid Events Services Imam Appointment Nikkah Education Services Alphabetland Sunday School Quran Learning Institute Community Services Social Services JMIC Assembly Hall Literary Social Media YouTube Channel Facebook Instagram Our App Photo Gallery Donate Funeral Services Contact Live Donate School Home Masjid Events Services Imam Appointment Nikkah Education Services Alphabetland Sunday School Quran Learning Institute Community Services Social Services JMIC Assembly Hall Literary Social Media YouTube Channel Facebook Instagram Our App Photo Gallery Donate Funeral Services Contact Welcome To Jam-e-Masjid Islamic Center The Jam-e-Masjid Islamic Center Inc., was established in May, 1988 as a non-profit religious educational organization to serve the Muslim community. It is a community-based organization depending on its volunteers to enable the development of a model Islamic society based on the guidance from the Quran and the Sunnah of Prophet Muhammad (peace and blessings be upon him) to maximize our reverence towards Allah (most gracious most merciful). The dedicated volunteers work in harmony towards the community’s efforts to: Build and sustain a progressive religious and cultural center for fulfilling our spiritual and social responsibilities. Build strong and cohesive relationships within the community irrespective of ethnicity, race, and religion. Facilitate Islamic education for children and adults. Enhance peace and justice. Develop a culture of caring. Mission Statement Help meet the religious, social, and cultural needs of the Muslim community, via the Sunday School, Alphabet-land, Quranic School, Salat-ul-Jumuah, Al-Ramadan, and Eid Programs. Events Calender About the Masjid Established in 1986, THE ISLAMIC SOCIETY OF MONMOUTH COUNTY (ISMC) is a non-profit organization with the objective of strengthening the bond of humanity. We are dedicated to creating an atmosphere for better citizenship and improving the well being of our community at large. Serving all those in need is a primary goal for our organization. Through our association with several humanitarian relief and development organizations, we are able to respond to human suffering in emergency and disaster situations at home and abroad. Get to Know us Our Services The Islamic Society of Monmouth County is committed to making all services required by the Muslim community easily accessible including but not limited to: Marriage Services Allah says in Qur’an, “And of His signs is that He created for you from yourselves mates that you may find tranquillity in them; and He placed between you affection and mercy. Indeed in that are signs for a people who give thought.” 30:21 Youth Programs Our goal is to engage today’s youth with engaging activities that help with building our future generation. For anyone that can't attend the community prayer at the park, there will be an early Eid prayer held at the masjid (109 E 24th St) at 7:30 AM. 3. We will pray taraweah tonight, Tuesday night. Taraweah/Isha at the masjid will start 7:30pm inshaAllah. Download the BayonneMuslims app for all prayer timings. title: Sunday School
description: The Jam-e-Masjid Islamic Center Inc., was established in May, 1988 as a non-profit religious educational organization to serve the Muslim community. It is a community-based organization depending on its volunteers to enable the development of a model Islamic society based on the guidance from the Quran and the Sunnah of Prophet Muhammad (peace and blessings be upon him) to maximize our reverence towards Allah (most gracious most merciful).
raw_text: Jam-e-Masjid Islamic Center Skip to content 📞 973-588-4059 📧 info@jmic.org Live Donate School Home Masjid Events Services Imam Appointment Nikkah Education Services Alphabetland Sunday School Quran Learning Institute Community Services Social Services JMIC Assembly Hall Literary Social Media YouTube Channel Facebook Instagram Our App Photo Gallery Donate Funeral Services Contact Live Donate School Home Masjid Events Services Imam Appointment Nikkah Education Services Alphabetland Sunday School Quran Learning Institute Community Services Social Services JMIC Assembly Hall Literary Social Media YouTube Channel Facebook Instagram Our App Photo Gallery Donate Funeral Services Contact Welcome To Jam-e-Masjid Islamic Center The Jam-e-Masjid Islamic Center Inc., was established in May, 1988 as a non-profit religious educational organization to serve the Muslim community. It is a community-based organization depending on its volunteers to enable the development of a model Islamic society based on the guidance from the Quran and the Sunnah of Prophet Muhammad (peace and blessings be upon him) to maximize our reverence towards Allah (most gracious most merciful). The dedicated volunteers work in harmony towards the community’s efforts to: Build and sustain a progressive religious and cultural center for fulfilling our spiritual and social responsibilities. Build strong and cohesive relationships within the community irrespective of ethnicity, race, and religion. Facilitate Islamic education for children and adults. Enhance peace and justice. Develop a culture of caring. Mission Statement Help meet the religious, social, and cultural needs of the Muslim community, via the Sunday School, Alphabet-land, Quranic School, Salat-ul-Jumuah, Al-Ramadan, and Eid Programs. Events Calender About the Masjid Established in 1986, THE ISLAMIC SOCIETY OF MONMOUTH COUNTY (ISMC) is a non-profit organization with the objective of strengthening the bond of humanity. We are dedicated to creating an atmosphere for better citizenship and improving the well being of our community at large. Serving all those in need is a primary goal for our organization. Through our association with several humanitarian relief and development organizations, we are able to respond to human suffering in emergency and disaster situations at home and abroad. Get to Know us Our Services The Islamic Society of Monmouth County is committed to making all services required by the Muslim community easily accessible including but not limited to: Marriage Services Allah says in Qur’an, “And of His signs is that He created for you from yourselves mates that you may find tranquillity in them; and He placed between you affection and mercy. Indeed in that are signs for a people who give thought.” 30:21 Youth Programs Our goal is to engage today’s youth with engaging activities that help with building our future generation. title: Sunday School
description: The Jam-e-Masjid Islamic Center Inc., was established in May, 1988 as a non-profit religious educational organization to serve the Muslim community. It is a community-based organization depending on its volunteers to enable the development of a model Islamic society based on the guidance from the Quran and the Sunnah of Prophet Muhammad (peace and blessings be upon him) to maximize our reverence towards Allah (most gracious most merciful).
raw_text: Jam-e-Masjid Islamic Center Skip to content 📞 973-588-4059 📧 info@jmic.org Live Donate School Home Masjid Events Services Imam Appointment Nikkah Education Services Alphabetland Sunday School Quran Learning Institute Community Services Social Services JMIC Assembly Hall Literary Social Media YouTube Channel Facebook Instagram Our App Photo Gallery Donate Funeral Services Contact Live Donate School Home Masjid Events Services Imam Appointment Nikkah Education Services Alphabetland Sunday School Quran Learning Institute Community Services Social Services JMIC Assembly Hall Literary Social Media YouTube Channel Facebook Instagram Our App Photo Gallery Donate Funeral Services Contact Welcome To Jam-e-Masjid Islamic Center The Jam-e-Masjid Islamic Center Inc., was established in May, 1988 as a non-profit religious educational organization to serve the Muslim community. It is a community-based organization depending on its volunteers to enable the development of a model Islamic society based on the guidance from the Quran and the Sunnah of Prophet Muhammad (peace and blessings be upon him) to maximize our reverence towards Allah (most gracious most merciful). The dedicated volunteers work in harmony towards the community’s efforts to: Build and sustain a progressive religious and cultural center for fulfilling our spiritual and social responsibilities. Build strong and cohesive relationships within the community irrespective of ethnicity, race, and religion. Facilitate Islamic education for children and adults. Enhance peace and justice. Develop a culture of caring. Mission Statement Help meet the religious, social, and cultural needs of the Muslim community, via the Sunday School, Alphabet-land, Quranic School, Salat-ul-Jumuah, Al-Ramadan, and Eid Programs. Events Calender About the Masjid Established in 1986, THE ISLAMIC SOCIETY OF MONMOUTH COUNTY (ISMC) is a non-profit organization with the objective of strengthening the bond of humanity. We are dedicated to creating an atmosphere for better citizenship and improving the well being of our community at large. Serving all those in need is a primary goal for our organization. Through our association with several humanitarian relief and development organizations, we are able to respond to human suffering in emergency and disaster situations at home and abroad. Get to Know us Our Services The Islamic Society of Monmouth County is committed to making all services required by the Muslim community easily accessible including but not limited to: Marriage Services Allah says in Qur’an, “And of His signs is that He created for you from yourselves mates that you may find tranquillity in them; and He placed between you affection and mercy. Indeed in that are signs for a people who give thought.” 30:21 Youth Programs Our goal is to engage today’s youth with engaging activities that help with building our future generation. title: Sunday School
description: The Jam-e-Masjid Islamic Center Inc., was established in May, 1988 as a non-profit religious educational organization to serve the Muslim community. It is a community-based organization depending on its volunteers to enable the development of a model Islamic society based on the guidance from the Quran and the Sunnah of Prophet Muhammad (peace and blessings be upon him) to maximize our reverence towards Allah (most gracious most merciful).
raw_text: Jam-e-Masjid Islamic Center Skip to content 📞 973-588-4059 📧 info@jmic.org Live Donate School Home Masjid Events Services Imam Appointment Nikkah Education Services Alphabetland Sunday School Quran Learning Institute Community Services Social Services JMIC Assembly Hall Literary Social Media YouTube Channel Facebook Instagram Our App Photo Gallery Donate Funeral Services Contact Live Donate School Home Masjid Events Services Imam Appointment Nikkah Education Services Alphabetland Sunday School Quran Learning Institute Community Services Social Services JMIC Assembly Hall Literary Social Media YouTube Channel Facebook Instagram Our App Photo Gallery Donate Funeral Services Contact Welcome To Jam-e-Masjid Islamic Center The Jam-e-Masjid Islamic Center Inc., was established in May, 1988 as a non-profit religious educational organization to serve the Muslim community. It is a community-based organization depending on its volunteers to enable the development of a model Islamic society based on the guidance from the Quran and the Sunnah of Prophet Muhammad (peace and blessings be upon him) to maximize our reverence towards Allah (most gracious most merciful). The dedicated volunteers work in harmony towards the community’s efforts to: Build and sustain a progressive religious and cultural center for fulfilling our spiritual and social responsibilities. Build strong and cohesive relationships within the community irrespective of ethnicity, race, and religion. Facilitate Islamic education for children and adults. Enhance peace and justice. Develop a culture of caring. Mission Statement Help meet the religious, social, and cultural needs of the Muslim community, via the Sunday School, Alphabet-land, Quranic School, Salat-ul-Jumuah, Al-Ramadan, and Eid Programs. Events Calender About the Masjid Established in 1986, THE ISLAMIC SOCIETY OF MONMOUTH COUNTY (ISMC) is a non-profit organization with the objective of strengthening the bond of humanity. We are dedicated to creating an atmosphere for better citizenship and improving the well being of our community at large. Serving all those in need is a primary goal for our organization. Through our association with several humanitarian relief and development organizations, we are able to respond to human suffering in emergency and disaster situations at home and abroad. Get to Know us Our Services The Islamic Society of Monmouth County is committed to making all services required by the Muslim community easily accessible including but not limited to: Marriage Services Allah says in Qur’an, “And of His signs is that He created for you from yourselves mates that you may find tranquillity in them; and He placed between you affection and mercy. Indeed in that are signs for a people who give thought.” 30:21 Youth Programs Our goal is to engage today’s youth with engaging activities that help with building our future generation. title: Sunday School
description: The Jam-e-Masjid Islamic Center Inc., was established in May, 1988 as a non-profit religious educational organization to serve the Muslim community. It is a community-based organization depending on its volunteers to enable the development of a model Islamic society based on the guidance from the Quran and the Sunnah of Prophet Muhammad (peace and blessings be upon him) to maximize our reverence towards Allah (most gracious most merciful).
raw_text: Jam-e-Masjid Islamic Center Skip to content 📞 973-588-4059 📧 info@jmic.org Live Donate School Home Masjid Events Services Imam Appointment Nikkah Education Services Alphabetland Sunday School Quran Learning Institute Community Services Social Services JMIC Assembly Hall Literary Social Media YouTube Channel Facebook Instagram Our App Photo Gallery Donate Funeral Services Contact Live Donate School Home Masjid Events Services Imam Appointment Nikkah Education Services Alphabetland Sunday School Quran Learning Institute Community Services Social Services JMIC Assembly Hall Literary Social Media YouTube Channel Facebook Instagram Our App Photo Gallery Donate Funeral Services Contact Welcome To Jam-e-Masjid Islamic Center The Jam-e-Masjid Islamic Center Inc., was established in May, 1988 as a non-profit religious educational organization to serve the Muslim community. It is a community-based organization depending on its volunteers to enable the development of a model Islamic society based on the guidance from the Quran and the Sunnah of Prophet Muhammad (peace and blessings be upon him) to maximize our reverence towards Allah (most gracious most merciful). The dedicated volunteers work in harmony towards the community’s efforts to: Build and sustain a progressive religious and cultural center for fulfilling our spiritual and social responsibilities. Build strong and cohesive relationships within the community irrespective of ethnicity, race, and religion. Facilitate Islamic education for children and adults. Enhance peace and justice. Develop a culture of caring. Mission Statement Help meet the religious, social, and cultural needs of the Muslim community, via the Sunday School, Alphabet-land, Quranic School, Salat-ul-Jumuah, Al-Ramadan, and Eid Programs. For anyone that can't attend the community prayer at the park, there will be an early Eid prayer held at the masjid (109 E 24th St) at 7:30 AM. 3. For this Friday, March 20th 2026, there will only be a single Jummah Khutbah at 1 PM. The masjid will return to the regular schedule of two Jummah Khutbahs next week. 19 Mar 2026 10:40 AM Jummah Times Jummah Prayer 1: 12:45PM Jummah Prayer 2: 1:45PM 12 Mar 2026 06:41 PM Welcoming Ramadan 2026 In accordance with AMJA and Bayonne Muslims global moon sighting policy, the moon was sighted and therefore InshaAllah the first day of Ramadan will be Wednesday, February 18th inshaAllah. We will pray taraweah tonight, Tuesday night. Taraweah/Isha at the masjid will start 7:30pm inshaAllah. Download the BayonneMuslims app for all prayer timings. Please come early and follow all masjid guidelines regarding parking and other safety precautions. title: Sunday School
description: The Jam-e-Masjid Islamic Center Inc., was established in May, 1988 as a non-profit religious educational organization to serve the Muslim community. It is a community-based organization depending on its volunteers to enable the development of a model Islamic society based on the guidance from the Quran and the Sunnah of Prophet Muhammad (peace and blessings be upon him) to maximize our reverence towards Allah (most gracious most merciful).
raw_text: Jam-e-Masjid Islamic Center Skip to content 📞 973-588-4059 📧 info@jmic.org Live Donate School Home Masjid Events Services Imam Appointment Nikkah Education Services Alphabetland Sunday School Quran Learning Institute Community Services Social Services JMIC Assembly Hall Literary Social Media YouTube Channel Facebook Instagram Our App Photo Gallery Donate Funeral Services Contact Live Donate School Home Masjid Events Services Imam Appointment Nikkah Education Services Alphabetland Sunday School Quran Learning Institute Community Services Social Services JMIC Assembly Hall Literary Social Media YouTube Channel Facebook Instagram Our App Photo Gallery Donate Funeral Services Contact Welcome To Jam-e-Masjid Islamic Center The Jam-e-Masjid Islamic Center Inc., was established in May, 1988 as a non-profit religious educational organization to serve the Muslim community. It is a community-based organization depending on its volunteers to enable the development of a model Islamic society based on the guidance from the Quran and the Sunnah of Prophet Muhammad (peace and blessings be upon him) to maximize our reverence towards Allah (most gracious most merciful). The dedicated volunteers work in harmony towards the community’s efforts to: Build and sustain a progressive religious and cultural center for fulfilling our spiritual and social responsibilities. Build strong and cohesive relationships within the community irrespective of ethnicity, race, and religion. Facilitate Islamic education for children and adults. Enhance peace and justice. Develop a culture of caring. Mission Statement Help meet the religious, social, and cultural needs of the Muslim community, via the Sunday School, Alphabet-land, Quranic School, Salat-ul-Jumuah, Al-Ramadan, and Eid Programs. title: Sunday School
description: The Jam-e-Masjid Islamic Center Inc., was established in May, 1988 as a non-profit religious educational organization to serve the Muslim community. It is a community-based organization depending on its volunteers to enable the development of a model Islamic society based on the guidance from the Quran and the Sunnah of Prophet Muhammad (peace and blessings be upon him) to maximize our reverence towards Allah (most gracious most merciful).

It is a community-based organization depending on its volunteers to enable the development of a model Islamic society based on the guidance from the Quran and the Sunnah of Prophet Muhammad (peace and blessings be upon him) to maximize our reverence towards Allah (most gracious most merciful). The dedicated volunteers work in harmony towards the community’s efforts to: Build and sustain a progressive religious and cultural center for fulfilling our spiritual and social responsibilities. Build strong and cohesive relationships within the community irrespective of ethnicity, race, and religion. Facilitate Islamic education for children and adults. Enhance peace and justice. Develop a culture of caring. Mission Statement Help meet the religious, social, and cultural needs of the Muslim community, via the Sunday School, Alphabet-land, Quranic School, Salat-ul-Jumuah, Al-Ramadan, and Eid Programs. Events Calender About the Masjid Established in 1986, THE ISLAMIC SOCIETY OF MONMOUTH COUNTY (ISMC) is a non-profit organization with the objective of strengthening the bond of humanity. We are dedicated to creating an atmosphere for better citizenship and improving the well being of our community at large. Serving all those in need is a primary goal for our organization. Through our association with several humanitarian relief and development organizations, we are able to respond to human suffering in emergency and disaster situations at home and abroad. Get to Know us Our Services The Islamic Society of Monmouth County is committed to making all services required by the Muslim community easily accessible including but not limited to: Marriage Services Allah says in Qur’an, “And of His signs is that He created for you from yourselves mates that you may find tranquillity in them; and He placed between you affection and mercy. Indeed in that are signs for a people who give thought.” 30:21 Youth Programs Our goal is to engage today’s youth with engaging activities that help with building our future generation. Events Calender About the Masjid Established in 1986, THE ISLAMIC SOCIETY OF MONMOUTH COUNTY (ISMC) is a non-profit organization with the objective of strengthening the bond of humanity. We are dedicated to creating an atmosphere for better citizenship and improving the well being of our community at large. Serving all those in need is a primary goal for our organization. Through our association with several humanitarian relief and development organizations, we are able to respond to human suffering in emergency and disaster situations at home and abroad. Get to Know us Our Services The Islamic Society of Monmouth County is committed to making all services required by the Muslim community easily accessible including but not limited to: Marriage Services Allah says in Qur’an, “And of His signs is that He created for you from yourselves mates that you may find tranquillity in them; and He placed between you affection and mercy. Indeed in that are signs for a people who give thought.” 30:21 Youth Programs Our goal is to engage today’s youth with engaging activities that help with building our future generation. It is held in the first Saturday of each month between 7:00pm and 9:00pm. It’s open for everyone, no reservation required. ICUC Outreach is dedicated in exemplifying good muslims values by serving our community. As muslims we set the best example for others to follow. At ICUC Outreach we are involved in improving connections with members of our local community, muslim and non-muslim. We are active members in political, interfaith and charity projects throughout the county and state. (We are currently constructing our outreach page. title: Join us for our third annual Darul Fath Arabic Intensive! Starting in June, this two month Arabic intensive will take beginners to intermediate level and intermediate students to an advanced level! We will study classical texts, grammar, morphology, and more, in this 2 month immersive experience! The timings are perfect for working professions and students as well!

Events Calender About the Masjid Established in 1986, THE ISLAMIC SOCIETY OF MONMOUTH COUNTY (ISMC) is a non-profit organization with the objective of strengthening the bond of humanity. We are dedicated to creating an atmosphere for better citizenship and improving the well being of our community at large. Serving all those in need is a primary goal for our organization. Through our association with several humanitarian relief and development organizations, we are able to respond to human suffering in emergency and disaster situations at home and abroad. Get to Know us Our Services The Islamic Society of Monmouth County is committed to making all services required by the Muslim community easily accessible including but not limited to: Marriage Services Allah says in Qur’an, “And of His signs is that He created for you from yourselves mates that you may find tranquillity in them; and He placed between you affection and mercy. Indeed in that are signs for a people who give thought.” 30:21 Youth Programs Our goal is to engage today’s youth with engaging activities that help with building our future generation. Join the Muslim Center of Greater Princeton on our journey to grow, empower, and serve our community Asalaamualaikum MCGP Family, Alhamdulillah, the MCGP team has worked diligently to clean the primary parking lot and 379 lot. While the spaces have been cleared, conditions remain less than ideal and parking capacity is limited. For safety reasons, we do not recommend full community attendance for Isha and Taraweeh at this time. Isha and a shortened 8 rakat Taraweeh will be conducted. We strongly encourage elders and children to remain at home. 🤲 Isha salaah will be at 8pm, inshallah This Ramadan, come pray, reflect, and grow together as one ummah. Events Calender About the Masjid Established in 1986, THE ISLAMIC SOCIETY OF MONMOUTH COUNTY (ISMC) is a non-profit organization with the objective of strengthening the bond of humanity. We are dedicated to creating an atmosphere for better citizenship and improving the well being of our community at large. Serving all those in need is a primary goal for our organization. Through our association with several humanitarian relief and development organizations, we are able to respond to human suffering in emergency and disaster situations at home and abroad. Get to Know us Our Services The Islamic Society of Monmouth County is committed to making all services required by the Muslim community easily accessible including but not limited to: Marriage Services Allah says in Qur’an, “And of His signs is that He created for you from yourselves mates that you may find tranquillity in them; and He placed between you affection and mercy. Indeed in that are signs for a people who give thought.” 30:21 Youth Programs Our goal is to engage today’s youth with engaging activities that help with building our future generation. Events Calender About the Masjid Established in 1986, THE ISLAMIC SOCIETY OF MONMOUTH COUNTY (ISMC) is a non-profit organization with the objective of strengthening the bond of humanity. We are dedicated to creating an atmosphere for better citizenship and improving the well being of our community at large. Serving all those in need is a primary goal for our organization. Through our association with several humanitarian relief and development organizations, we are able to respond to human suffering in emergency and disaster situations at home and abroad. Get to Know us Our Services The Islamic Society of Monmouth County is committed to making all services required by the Muslim community easily accessible including but not limited to: Marriage Services Allah says in Qur’an, “And of His signs is that He created for you from yourselves mates that you may find tranquillity in them; and He placed between you affection and mercy. Indeed in that are signs for a people who give thought.” 30:21 Youth Programs Our goal is to engage today’s youth with engaging activities that help with building our future generation. ICUC Outreach is dedicated in exemplifying good muslims values by serving our community. As muslims we set the best example for others to follow. At ICUC Outreach we are involved in improving connections with members of our local community, muslim and non-muslim. Events Calender About the Masjid Established in 1986, THE ISLAMIC SOCIETY OF MONMOUTH COUNTY (ISMC) is a non-profit organization with the objective of strengthening the bond of humanity. We are dedicated to creating an atmosphere for better citizenship and improving the well being of our community at large. Serving all those in need is a primary goal for our organization. Through our association with several humanitarian relief and development organizations, we are able to respond to human suffering in emergency and disaster situations at home and abroad. Get to Know us Our Services The Islamic Society of Monmouth County is committed to making all services required by the Muslim community easily accessible including but not limited to: Marriage Services Allah says in Qur’an, “And of His signs is that He created for you from yourselves mates that you may find tranquillity in them; and He placed between you affection and mercy. Indeed in that are signs for a people who give thought.” 30:21 Youth Programs Our goal is to engage today’s youth with engaging activities that help with building our future generation. It is held in the first Saturday of each month between 7:00pm and 9:00pm. It’s open for everyone, no reservation required. ICUC Outreach is dedicated in exemplifying good muslims values by serving our community. As muslims we set the best example for others to follow. At ICUC Outreach we are involved in improving connections with members of our local community, muslim and non-muslim. We are active members in political, interfaith and charity projects throughout the county and state. (We are currently constructing our outreach page. It is held in the first Saturday of each month between 7:00pm and 9:00pm. It’s open for everyone, no reservation required. ICUC Outreach is dedicated in exemplifying good muslims values by serving our community. As muslims we set the best example for others to follow. At ICUC Outreach we are involved in improving connections with members of our local community, muslim and non-muslim. We are active members in political, interfaith and charity projects throughout the county and state. (We are currently constructing our outreach page. title: Alhamdulillah 🌙✨
description: 169 likes, 3 comments - icpcnj on April 6, 2026: "Alhamdulillah 🌙✨ We were honored to host the Ramadan Qur’an Competition Awards Ceremony at ICPC Paterson on March 28th. It was a beautiful gathering celebrating dedication perseverance and love for the Book of Allah We were also blessed by the presence of our respected shuyookh whose attendance added honor and inspiration to the evening Congratulations to all of our participants and winners your hard work and commitment to the Qur’an are truly inspiring. May Allah continue to bless you increase you in knowledge and make the Qur’an the light of your hearts A heartfelt thank you to our organizers judges and families who supported this meaningful event #Ramadan2026 #QuranCompetition #ICPCPaterson #PeopleOfQuran #LoveForQuran".

title: Surah Maryam is a powerful and deeply moving surah that reveals how Allah supports His servants in their most difficult and lonely moments. Through its stories, we witness what it truly means to rely on Him, remain steadfast, and hold firmly to faith no matter the circumstances.
description: 22 likes, 0 comments - icpcnj on April 8, 2026: "Surah Maryam is a powerful and deeply moving surah that reveals how Allah supports His servants in their most difficult and lonely moments. Through its stories, we witness what it truly means to rely on Him, remain steadfast, and hold firmly to faith no matter the circumstances. This surah invites us to reflect on our relationship with Allah, how we turn to Him in hardship, and how certain we are in His promise. Join us for this Tafseer Series with Sh. Ismail Hamdi Wednesdays after Maghrib ICPC – Younis Register now: www.icpcnj.org/courses #suratmaryam #tafseer".
